originalTitle: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
title: Super News! South Africa's President Cyril Ramaphosa is Staying!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly, super-duper news reporter here, ready to share some exciting news from the beautiful country of South Africa! Get ready to clap your hands and smile!

Meet Our President Friend!

In South Africa, there is a very important person who leads the whole country. He is like the captain of a big, big ship, or the head teacher of a very, very big school! His name is President Cyril Ramaphosa. He has a very big job looking after all the people and making sure the country grows strong and happy. He works hard every single day to make South Africa a great place for everyone. He makes big plans and tries to help all the children, parents, and grandparents in the country. He is a busy, busy leader!

Did You Know?

Did you know that South Africa is a country full of wonders? It has beautiful beaches, giant mountains, and amazing wildlife like lions, elephants, and giraffes! People there speak many different languages, which is very cool. It's a land of sunshine and bright smiles!

What's All the Big Talk About?

Sometimes, grown-ups talk a lot about important things, especially when it comes to leaders. Recently, there has been a lot of talking and wondering in South Africa. Some grown-ups were asking a big question: would President Ramaphosa stay in his important job, or would he leave? It was like a big puzzle that everyone was trying to solve! People were discussing it in their homes, in the newspapers, and on the radio. Everyone was curious about what would happen next with their country's leader.

President Ramaphosa Says: "I Am Staying!"

Guess what, my awesome friends? On Monday evening, President Ramaphosa gave a very clear and important answer! He stood up tall and said, "No, I am not leaving my job! I am staying!" He made a big decision to continue leading the country. This means he will keep working hard for all the people of South Africa. He wants to finish the important plans he has for the country. It was a very important message that made many people happy!

Why Were People Asking These Big Questions?

Now, you might be wondering why people were asking if he would leave. Well, sometimes when people have very big jobs, like being a president, there can be big questions and discussions about things that happen. Grown-ups want to make sure everyone follows the rules and does their job in the best way. There were some big questions and lots of talks happening among grown-ups about something that happened. These talks made some people wonder if President Ramaphosa should step down from his role. "Step down" means to leave a job. But the President listened to everyone, thought very carefully, and decided his work is not done yet. He chose to stay and keep leading his country forward.

What Does a President Do All Day?

Being a president is not just about making speeches! It's a very busy job with many responsibilities. A president helps make sure that all the children can go to good schools and learn amazing things. They also try to make sure there are enough jobs for grown-ups so families can live comfortably. Presidents work to keep the country safe and peaceful for everyone. They also meet with other important leaders from different countries. This helps South Africa be good friends with other nations around the world. It's a really big and important job that needs a lot of thinking and planning!

Did You Know?

Did you know that in many countries, including South Africa, grown-ups get to choose their leader? They do this by something called 'voting'! It's like everyone choosing the captain of their team, but for the whole country. Each grown-up gets to pick who they think will be the best leader for everyone. It's a very special way to choose who will lead!

Why Is It Good That He Stays?

When a leader like President Ramaphosa stays in his job, it can be very good for the country. It means he can keep working on all the important plans he has started. It helps the country stay strong and steady, like a big, stable tree! Everyone knows who is in charge, and they can keep working together towards common goals. It helps the country feel peaceful and allows big projects to get finished. He wants to keep helping South Africa grow bigger, stronger, and even more wonderful for all its people.

What Happens Next?

Now that President Ramaphosa has decided to stay, he will continue his very important work. He will keep trying his best to make South Africa a fantastic place for every child, every family, and every animal too! Your friendly reporter will keep watching and sharing more super news with you! So, let's cheer for South Africa and its leader as they continue their journey! Keep smiling and keep learning, amazing African kids!
summary: South Africa's President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced he will not leave his big job and will continue to lead the country, after much talk and questions among grown-ups.
whyMatters: This is important because when a leader decides to stay, it helps the country remain stable and allows them to continue working on important plans for everyone, ensuring schools are good and people are safe.

originalTitle: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
title: Big News from Uganda: President Museveni is Leader Again!
content:
Hello, amazing children all across Africa and the world! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, bringing you special news from our wonderful continent. Today, we're going on a little adventure to a beautiful country called Uganda. Have you ever heard of Uganda? It’s a very special place in East Africa, known for its green hills, big lakes, and amazing animals!

Meet the President of Uganda

In every country, there's a leader who helps everyone. In Uganda, this leader is called the President. His name is Yoweri Museveni. He is a very, very important man in Uganda. Imagine your school has a head teacher who helps everyone learn and keeps the school safe. Well, a president is like the head teacher for the whole country! President Museveni is 81 years old. That's a lot of birthdays! If you are 7 years old, he is like having your birthday 11 more times than you have had! He has been the leader of Uganda for a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? He has been the president for 40 years! That's even longer than most of your parents have been alive! Imagine being a little baby, growing up, going to school, and then being a grown-up, and still, the same person is the president. That's how long 40 years is in Uganda!

How Do Leaders Get Chosen?

Sometimes, grown-ups in a country get to choose their leader. This choosing is called an 'election'. It's like when you have to choose which game to play with your friends, but it's much bigger! In an election, grown-ups go to special places and put a mark next to the person they want to be the leader. It’s their way of showing who they think will do the best job for the country. In January, grown-ups in Uganda had an election. They voted for who they wanted to be their president. And President Museveni was chosen to be the leader again. This is very big news!

A Special Number: Seven Times!

This is not the first time President Museveni has been chosen. He has now been chosen for the seventh time! Can you imagine having seven birthday cakes? Or getting to play your favourite game seven times in a row? Seven times is a big number! Each time a president is chosen, it's called a 'term'. So, President Museveni has been sworn in for his seventh 'term' as president. This means he will lead the country for another few years. It's a very big moment for Uganda because it means he has been president for a very long time, and now he will continue.

What Does a President Do?

What does a president do all day? Well, it's a very important job! A president helps to make sure everyone in the country is safe and happy. They help build roads so cars can drive easily, and they help build schools so children like you can learn wonderful new things. They also help to build hospitals so that if anyone gets sick, they can get better. Presidents also look after the country's amazing nature, like the big lakes and the beautiful animals. Uganda has big national parks where you can see elephants, lions, and even special mountain gorillas! *Did you know?* Uganda is often called the 'Pearl of Africa' because it's so beautiful with all its green lands and rich wildlife!

A Little Bit About the Choosing

Sometimes, when there's a big election, not everyone agrees about how fair the choosing was. It's like when you're playing a game, and some friends might think the rules weren't followed perfectly. In Uganda, some people felt that the voting was not perfect. They felt there could have been improvements in how the choosing happened. But in the end, President Museveni was declared the winner, and now he has officially started his new time as president. He took a special promise to lead the country well. This means he will continue to make important decisions for all the people of Uganda, including all the children.

Hopes for Uganda's Future

When a leader starts a new term, everyone in the country hopes for good things. They hope for peace, so everyone can live without worry. They hope for better schools, so children can grow up smart and strong. They hope for happy families and enough food for everyone. *Did you know?* Uganda is home to Lake Victoria, which is the largest lake in Africa and a very important source of water for many people! As your news reporter, I wish all the children of Uganda a bright future, filled with learning, laughter, and lots of fun! It's a very exciting time for the country, and we hope President Museveni will lead them to many more happy days. Let’s all send our good wishes to Uganda from across our big African family! *Did you know?* Uganda has many different kinds of fruits like mangoes, pineapples, and bananas that grow right in their country! Yum!
summary: Yoweri Museveni, 81, has become the President of Uganda for the seventh time after recent elections, extending his leadership of the country for 40 years.
whyMatters: This is important because President Museveni has been the leader for a very, very long time, and now he will continue to make big decisions for all the people of Uganda, including making sure kids have schools and a safe place to live.

originalTitle: France seeks to move beyond colonial ties by meeting African leaders in Kenya
title: Africa and France: New Friends for a Bright Future!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends in Africa! I'm your super kid reporter, and I have some very exciting news to share with you today. Something big and important is happening that will help make our beautiful continent even better!

A Very Special Meeting in Kenya

Imagine a big meeting, like when your teachers and principal talk about important school plans. Well, grown-ups called 'leaders' have been having a very important meeting! These aren't just any leaders. They are leaders from many different countries in Africa and the leader of France. They met in a super cool country called Kenya. Did you know? Kenya is famous for its amazing wildlife, like giant elephants and tall giraffes! It's a beautiful part of Africa.

Who Are These Leaders?

When we say 'leaders,' we mean the people who help make big decisions for their whole country. They are like the head of a very big family. African leaders work hard to make life good for all the people in their countries, especially children like you! The leader of France also works hard for his country. They all want to see happy people and healthy communities.

What Does 'Colonial Ties' Mean?

Now, this is a bit of a grown-up word, but I'll make it super simple. A long, long time ago, even before your grandparents were born, some countries in Europe, like France, were in charge of many countries in Africa. It was like one big brother telling a smaller brother what to do, all the time. This was called 'colonial times.' But guess what? African countries grew up and became free! Hooray! They wanted to make their own choices and be in charge of their own homes. So, they said, 'We want to be free and decide for ourselves!' And they did! Now, France wants to be a new kind of friend. Not like the 'big brother' from long ago, but like a true, equal friend who plays fair. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent with 54 different countries! Each one is special and unique.

New Thinking for New Friendship

This meeting in Kenya is super special because it shows that France wants to change how it thinks about Africa. For many years, these kinds of meetings happened, but France was usually seen as the one making all the plans. Now, it's different! This is the first time since the 1970s that they are meeting like this. It means they want to be real partners. What's a partner? A partner is someone you work with side-by-side. Like when you work with a friend on a school project, you both share ideas and help each other. No one is the boss; everyone is a team!

What Did They Talk About?

At this big meeting, the African leaders and the French leader talked about many important things. Imagine all the things that make your life good, or things you wish were even better! They talked about how to: * Build more wonderful schools so every child can learn and play. * Have more hospitals so everyone can stay healthy and strong. * Make sure everyone has clean water to drink every day. * Grow yummy food so no one goes hungry. * Create good jobs for grown-ups so families can be happy. * Help keep our beautiful planet clean and healthy. They talked about things like stopping plastic pollution and planting more trees. Did you know? Trees help us breathe clean air! They are like giant air cleaners for our planet.

Why This Is Good News for You!

When countries are good friends and work together as partners, amazing things can happen! More help means more schools, better doctors, and more fun things for kids to do. It means a brighter future for all children in Africa. You'll have more chances to learn, to grow, and to follow your biggest dreams. It’s like when all the players on a soccer team work together; they score more goals! When countries work together, they can make life better for everyone. Did you know? France has a very famous tower called the Eiffel Tower, and it's super tall!

A Bright Future Ahead

So, this meeting in Kenya is a really big deal. It's about building strong, new friendships between Africa and France. It's about being equal partners and helping each other grow. This is very good news for our amazing continent and for all of you, the future leaders of Africa! Let's cheer for friendship and teamwork! We are ready for a wonderful future filled with hope and happiness!
summary: African leaders and the French leader met in Kenya to start a new, equal friendship, moving past old 'colonial ties' to work together on important things like schools, health, and a clean planet.
whyMatters: This meeting is important because it means countries want to be fair friends and help each other, which will bring more schools, hospitals, clean water, and good jobs to Africa, making life better for kids like you!

originalTitle: Can China counter US sanctions with its trade power?
title: America and China: A Big Game of Buying and Selling!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! I'm Kofi the Kudu Kid Reporter, and today we're going to talk about something super interesting happening far, far away! It's like a big game between two giant countries.

America and China: Two Big Countries


Imagine two very, very big countries on our planet. One is called America, and the other is called China. They are like two giant friends, but sometimes, they play a bit of a tricky game when it comes to money and things they buy and sell.

America is a country with lots of tall buildings, fast cars, and people who invent many cool things. China is also a very, very big country, with many, many people and clever minds that make countless toys, clothes, and electronics.

These two countries make tons of things. They also buy and sell lots of things to other countries all over the world. This buying and selling is called 'trade.' It's like going to your local market, but on a super-duper giant scale!

Did you know? China has the most people in the whole world! America is also a very big country with many different kinds of people living there.

What is a "Money Game"?


So, America and China are having a bit of a "money game." It's not a game with balls, skipping ropes, or your favourite toy cars. It's a game about who sells more things and who is very strong with their businesses.

Sometimes, countries don't always agree on how to play this game. They both want to be the best at making things and selling things to other countries.

This competition means they are always looking for ways to be number one in the world of trade. It's like when you and your friend both want to be the best at drawing or running!

What are "Sanctions"? (A Timeout for Business!)


Now, imagine your friend does something that makes you a little upset. Maybe they broke your crayon, or they didn't share their sweets. You might say, "I'm not playing with you for a little while!"

When big countries do this, it's called "sanctions." It means one country says, "Because we don't like what you are doing, we won't buy your special things, or we won't sell you our special things."

For example, America has sometimes said, "We won't let our people buy certain things from China." They do this to try and make China change something they are doing. It's like giving them a timeout to think about their actions.

It's a way for countries to talk without using angry words or fighting. They hope the other country will listen and change.

Did you know? Countries use sanctions because they believe it's a way to talk without fighting with soldiers. It's a way to say, "Please change!" without causing more trouble.

China's "Trade Power" (Selling Lots of Things!)


China makes so many things that we use every single day! Think about your toys, some of your clothes, or even parts of your parents' phone. Many of these things come from China.

Because China sells so many, many things to countries all over the world, it has a lot of "trade power." "Trade power" means they are very, very good at buying and selling. They have a big market for their goods.

If China stops selling things, many other countries might miss those things a lot. So, China can also say, "If you do that, maybe we won't sell you our things either!"

It's like having many delicious sweets, and everyone wants to buy them from you. You have power because people really want your sweets! This gives China a strong voice in the "money game."

Did you know? Many, many things in your house probably have a "Made in China" label on them! China is often called "the factory of the world" because they make so much.

Special Rocks: "Rare Earths"


Now, let's talk about something super, super special that comes from deep in the ground. They are called "rare earths." No, they are not from space! They are special kinds of rocks and minerals.

These rocks are not everywhere. They are "rare," which means they are hard to find and mine. So, not every country has them.

Why are they so important? Because we need them to make amazing modern things! Like your parents' fancy phone, your computer tablet, or even the new electric cars that don't need petrol.

Guess what? China has a lot of these special "rare earth" rocks! More than any other country in the whole world! So, if America needs these rocks for their phones and cars, they often have to buy them from China.

This gives China even more "trade power." It's like if you had the only special crayon that could draw a rainbow, everyone would want to borrow it from you! Having these rare earths makes China a very important player in the world.

Did you know? Without "rare earths," many of our cool gadgets like smartphones, smart TVs, and even wind turbines (which make clean electricity) would not work!

Why This "Money Game" Matters to Us in Africa


You might be thinking, "Kofi, America and China are so far, far away! Why should I care about their money game?"

Well, even though they are far away from our beautiful continent, what happens between them can affect us a little bit. When these big countries have a "money game," it can change how much things cost everywhere in the world.

For example, if things cost more to make because of their competition, then they might cost more to buy here in our shops too. Or, if they stop selling certain things to each other, our countries might have fewer things to buy or they might become more expensive.

Our countries in Africa also buy many things from China and America, and we sell things to them too! So, it's good for everyone when these big countries play fair and are friends, even when they are competing.

When big countries are happy and work together, it's usually better for everyone in the world, including us right here in Africa. We want a world where everyone can trade fairly and grow.

Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, and we trade with countries all over the world, buying and selling many different kinds of goods!

Playing Nicely Together


So, America and China are in a big "money game." They are competing about who is the best at making and selling things to the world.

America sometimes uses "sanctions," which is like a timeout for businesses. And China uses its "trade power" by selling lots of things and having special rocks like "rare earths."

It's a big, grown-up competition, but we all hope they can find a way to work together and be fair. Just like in our playground, it's always best when everyone can share, play nicely, and respect each other.

That way, we all get to have the things we need and want, and the world stays happy and fair for everyone! It's important for big countries to set a good example for the rest of us.

originalTitle: The crisis is Sudan is much worse than what is acknowledged
title: The crisis is Sudan is much worse than what is acknowledged
content: Even in Khartoum, where there is no longer fighting, the humanitarian situation is dire.

originalTitle: Over 370 Afghans killed in Pakistan conflict in first 3 months of 2026: UN
title: News From Far Away: Wishing for Peace for All!
content:
Hello From Your African Reporter!

Hello, my wonderful friends from all over Africa! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter, ready to share some important news with you today. We talk about things happening in our big, beautiful world. Sometimes the news is happy, and sometimes it makes us think about how we can help make things better. Today, we are talking about news from a place very far away. It's a country called Afghanistan. It's next to another country called Pakistan. These countries are not in Africa, but they are part of our big world family. And what happens there can make us all wish for peace.

News From Far, Far Away

Imagine two big groups of people. Sometimes, these groups do not agree with each other. When people don't agree and don't talk nicely, sometimes they start to fight. This is what has been happening in Afghanistan and Pakistan. It's like when you and your friend might disagree about a game. But this kind of fighting is much, much bigger and very serious. It's not a game at all. It makes many people sad and scared. Did you know? Afghanistan is a country with many tall mountains! It's famous for its beautiful carpets and delicious dried fruits, like raisins and apricots.

What is 'Fighting'?

When we say 'fighting' in this news, it means grown-ups using big, loud things. They use things that make big noises and can hurt people. This is called 'conflict.' Conflict is when people are seriously against each other. They use force instead of talking. This fighting has been going on for some time. It became more serious around February. Imagine loud noises, like very big fireworks, but not for fun. These noises can scare animals and people, especially children. Did you know? The opposite of fighting is peace! Peace means everyone is happy, safe, and friendly together. No loud noises, just smiles and sharing!

Who are the Taliban and Pakistani Forces?

On one side, there is a group called the Taliban. The Taliban are a group of people who want to be in charge of Afghanistan. They have different ideas about how the country should be run. Sometimes, they use fighting to try and get what they want. On the other side are the Pakistani forces. These are like the brave police and army people from Pakistan. Their job is to protect their country and its people. They try to keep everyone safe. So, it's like two different teams. But instead of playing a game, they are having a very serious disagreement. And sometimes, this disagreement turns into fighting. Did you know? Pakistan is a country with busy cities and also big, dry deserts! They speak many languages there, including Urdu.

What are 'Air Raids'?

Sometimes, this fighting happens from the sky. This is called an 'air raid.' Air raids mean that big airplanes fly very high. From these airplanes, sometimes things are dropped that make huge bangs when they hit the ground. These bangs are very loud and can break things. Imagine a very loud thunderclap, but even louder and closer. It's a very scary sound. These air raids can make people's homes shake. They can make everyone feel very unsafe. Did you know? Birds are amazing flyers! They can fly very high in the sky, much higher than any airplane, but they don't make scary noises, just beautiful songs!

Who Gets Hurt?

When there is fighting and air raids, sadly, many people get hurt. Not just the grown-ups who are fighting. Many, many regular people, like moms, dads, grandmas, grandpas, and even children, can get hurt or lose their homes. Reports say that in just three months, sadly, hundreds of people have lost their lives. That's a very big number of friends, families, and neighbours. The United Nations, a big group that helps the world, told us this sad news. Imagine your whole street of friends. A big number of people like that have been affected. This is why we wish so hard for peace. When people fight, it makes everyone very, very sad. Did you know? Every child, no matter where they live, has a right to be safe and to play and learn without fear. This is very important!

What is the United Nations?

The United Nations, or UN for short, is like a really big club for almost all the countries in the world. Imagine all your friends from different schools coming together to help each other. That's what the UN does. They try to stop fighting. They help people who are hurt or who have lost their homes. The UN also tells the world what is happening, like the sad news from Afghanistan and Pakistan. They want everyone to live in peace. Did you know? The UN has a special day called 'International Day of Peace' every year on September 21st! On this day, everyone thinks about peace and how to be kind.

Why is Peace Important?

Peace is important because it means everyone can live safely. It means children can go to school and play outside without being scared. It means families can stay together in their homes. When there is peace, people can build new things, learn new things, and help each other. When there is fighting, all those good things become very hard. People are too busy trying to stay safe. We all want to live in a world where everyone is kind to each other. We want to share and play, not fight and be scared. This is why news like this makes us think about peace even more. Did you know? Many countries in Africa, like Kenya and South Africa, also work together with the UN to help make the world a better place!

Our Big Wish for Everyone

So, my dear friends, this news from far away reminds us of something very important. We must always try to talk kindly and share with each other. We must try to solve problems without fighting. We hope and wish with all our hearts that the people in Afghanistan and Pakistan will find peace very, very soon. We wish for all the children there to be safe and happy again. Just like we want for all children in Africa and all over the world. Let's all send our kind thoughts and wishes for peace to Afghanistan and Pakistan. Remember, every little bit of kindness helps our world become a happier place. Thank you for listening, and keep spreading peace!
summary: Fighting in far-away countries Afghanistan and Pakistan has made many people, including children, very sad and unsafe, and everyone hopes for peace soon.
whyMatters: This matters because fighting makes many children and families sad and scared, and we all want people everywhere to be safe and live in peace.

originalTitle: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
title: Working for Peace: A Big Talk is Happening Far Away!
content:
Welcome, Super Kids!

Hello, my wonderful African friends! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news from far, far away. Today, we're talking about something very grown-up, but it's super important for everyone in the whole world! It's about countries trying to make peace and stop fighting. Just like when you and your friend might have a small disagreement, sometimes big countries have disagreements too. And just like you might try to talk it out, countries try to talk it out as well. This news is about two countries, America and Iran, and how they are trying to be friends again. It's a bit like a big puzzle they are trying to solve together. Everyone hopes they can find the best way to live happily side-by-side without any problems.

What is a "Ceasefire"?

Imagine you are playing a game with your friends, maybe football or tag. Sometimes, during the game, you might need a break, right? You might say, "Time out!" or "Let's pause for a moment." A ceasefire is a bit like that, but for big countries. It means they agree to stop fighting each other for a little while. They promise to put down their weapons and not hurt anyone. This break gives them a chance to talk and try to find a way to stop fighting for good. It's a very important first step towards making real peace. It's like pressing the pause button on a video game, hoping that when you press play again, everyone can be friends. A ceasefire helps people feel safer for a little while, hoping for true peace to come soon. Did you know? The word "ceasefire" means to stop (cease) shooting guns (fire)! It's a special word for stopping fighting.

A Big Problem Far Away

Now, let's talk about the news. There are two big countries far away from us: America and Iran. For some time, they have had some problems. They agreed to have a ceasefire, which means they promised to stop fighting for a whole month. That's a very long time! Everyone was happy that they were trying to make peace. They hoped this break would help them talk things over and become friends again. But recently, the leader of America, who is called the President, said something that made people a little worried. He said that this ceasefire, this special break from fighting, was becoming "unbelievably weak." This means it's not very strong anymore, and it might not last. It's like a bridge that feels wobbly and might break.

What Does "Life Support" Mean?

This is where it gets a bit tricky, but I'll explain! The President of America also said the ceasefire was "on massive life support." What does that mean? Imagine if someone was very, very sick in a hospital. Sometimes, doctors use special machines to help them breathe or keep their heart beating. Those machines are called "life support." They help a person stay alive when they are very weak. So, when the President said the ceasefire was "on massive life support," he meant that the promise to stop fighting was very weak. It was barely staying alive, and it might end soon if they don't find a better way to agree. It means the talks are not going well, and the hope for peace is very fragile, like a tiny bird's egg. Did you know? Leaders of countries have very important jobs. They have to make big decisions that affect everyone in their country, and sometimes, the whole world!

Why is Talking Important?

When countries have problems, the best way to solve them is by talking. Just like when you have a problem with a friend, you talk about it instead of hitting or yelling, right? The countries, America and Iran, have been talking. Iran even gave a new idea, like a new plan, to try and fix things. We call this a "counteroffer." But the President of America didn't like this new plan very much. He thought it wasn't good enough to make the peace strong. So, they are still trying to find a plan that both countries will be happy with. It's like two friends trying to decide what game to play, and they both need to agree. It's hard work, but it's the only way to find a peaceful solution for everyone. Talking helps us understand each other better, even if we don't always agree at first.

Wishing for Peace

Even though this news sounds a little sad because the ceasefire is weak, we must remember that people are still trying. Leaders are still talking and working hard to find a way for America and Iran to live in peace. Every child, every family, and every country in the world hopes for peace. Fighting hurts everyone and stops people from living happy, safe lives. We hope that America and Iran can find a good solution very soon. We hope they can make their ceasefire strong again, or even better, find a way for lasting peace. We will keep listening and hoping for good news, because a world where everyone is friends is the best world for all of us. Let's send our good thoughts to them, hoping they find peace soon! We all want to live in a world where smiles are everywhere and no one has to worry about fighting. Did you know? Many countries around the world have special groups that help other countries talk and try to make peace. They are like friendly mediators!
summary: The leader of America says a special break from fighting between America and Iran, called a ceasefire, is very weak and might end soon because they are finding it hard to agree on a plan for peace.
whyMatters: This news is important because when big countries fight, it can make the whole world worry, and everyone wants countries to be friends and live in peace so that people everywhere can be safe and happy.

originalTitle: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
title: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
content: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule.

originalTitle: Canvas hack: company pays criminals to delete students' stolen data
title: Good News! A Big School Website Fixed a Sneaky Problem!
content:
Jambo, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly reporter, Auntie Ama, bringing you important news from around the world. Today, we're talking about computers and keeping our secrets safe. You know how you use computers for games or learning? Well, grown-up students use them for school too. A big website they use had a little hiccup, but don't worry, they are fixing it! Sometimes, things can go wrong on the internet. But it's good to know that clever grown-ups are always working hard to make things right. Let's learn about what happened and why it's a happy ending for many students.

What is Canvas? (And What is a Website?)

Imagine you have a big, special notebook where all your school work lives. It has your drawings, your sums, and even your teacher's notes. Now, imagine that notebook is on a computer, and many older students use it! That's a bit like 'Canvas'. Canvas is a super big website that helps older students in universities and colleges with their lessons. These are big schools for grown-ups! They log in to find their homework, see their grades, and talk to their teachers. It's like a big online classroom for thousands of students all over the world! *Did you know?* The internet is like a giant spiderweb connecting computers all around the world, letting us share information and learn new things!

Who Are "Hackers"? (And Why They're Not Very Nice)

Now, sometimes, there are people who like to play tricks. Not fun tricks, but sneaky computer tricks. We call them 'hackers'. Hackers are people who try to get into computers or websites without being asked. It's like someone trying to sneak into your house without permission! They shouldn't do that, right? They might try to look at things that aren't theirs, or even take some information that doesn't belong to them. These hackers found a way to get into the Canvas website. They didn't steal money from banks, but they did try to take some important information about the students who use Canvas. This caused a big problem! *Did you know?* Many computer people work very hard to make sure our websites are like strong castles with big, locked gates to keep bad guys out!

What Kind of "Secrets" Did They Try to Take?

So, what kind of 'secrets' or 'information' did these sneaky hackers try to take? It's not like they took your favourite toy! They were looking for things like student names, what lessons they were taking, or maybe even some things about their homework. This kind of information is called 'data'. Data is just a fancy word for all the little bits of information we put into computers. It's important to keep our data safe, just like you keep your drawings safe in your school bag so no one can mess them up. Imagine if someone took your name and your favourite colour without asking. It wouldn't feel very good, would it? That's why it's a big deal when hackers try to take student data. *Did you know?* Every time you play a game on a computer or tablet, you are using 'data'! It helps the game remember your score and what level you are on.

The Company Made a Special Deal

The company that made Canvas is called Instructure. When they found out about the sneaky hackers, they got to work super fast! They wanted to make sure all the students' information was safe again. So, what did they do? They made a very special 'agreement' or a 'deal' with the hackers. This was a very hard choice for the company. They decided to give a special kind of payment to the hackers. This wasn't because they liked the hackers, no! It was to make sure the hackers would really delete all the information they took and promise not to keep it. It was like saying, 'Here, take this, but you *must* throw away our students' precious information and promise never to bother them again.' The company wanted to be super sure that all the student secrets were safe and gone forever from the bad guys' computers. It was a way to protect the students as fast as possible and make sure their private information couldn't be used for bad things. They cared a lot about keeping their students' secrets safe. *Did you know?* Sometimes, talking nicely and making a promise can solve a big problem, even if it's with people who did something wrong!

Keeping Our Own Secrets Safe (Online Safety)

This story reminds us that it's good to be careful with our secrets, especially when we are using computers or tablets. When you are old enough to use websites that ask for your name or other information, always remember these simple rules: 1. **Ask a grown-up:** Always ask your mum, dad, or teacher before you share any information online. They are there to help you stay safe and make good choices! 2. **Strong passwords:** Grown-ups use secret words called 'passwords' to log into websites. If you ever use one, it should be like a super-strong secret code that no one else can guess! 3. **Don't click on strange links:** If you see a button or a link on a website that looks weird or says something strange, don't click it! It might be a trick to get your information. 4. **Think before you share:** Once something is on the internet, it's very hard to take it back. So, always think if it's okay to share something with the whole world. *Did you know?* Sharing your secrets online without thinking is like shouting them out in a big, crowded market – everyone might hear them!

Why This News Matters to Us!

Even if you are not using Canvas yet, this story is important for all of us here in Africa. Many schools and kids are starting to use computers and the internet more and more for learning. It shows us that big companies like Instructure work very hard to protect our information. It also teaches us to be smart and careful when we use computers. Just like you learn to cross the road safely, we also need to learn to be safe online. It's about being clever and asking for help when we need it. Our world is full of amazing technology, and by being smart, we can use it for all sorts of wonderful things, like learning about different animals or playing fun educational games! *Did you know?* Many schools in Africa are getting new computers and tablets to help children learn even better and explore the world!

Good News for Students!

So, what's the happy ending to our story? The company, Instructure, did a great job. They made sure the sneaky hackers deleted all the student information. This means that the students' secrets are safe again, and they can go back to using Canvas for their lessons without worrying. Phew! It's good to know that there are grown-ups and companies working hard to keep our online world safe and sound. We should always remember that being kind and safe online makes the internet a better place for everyone. Keep learning, keep exploring, and keep those big smiles on your faces! This is Auntie Ama, your kid-friendly reporter, signing off! See you next time for more amazing news!
summary: A big school website called Canvas had a problem with sneaky hackers, but the company made a special deal to make sure students' secrets are safe again.
whyMatters: This story helps us learn to be careful with our secrets when we use computers, and it shows that companies work hard to keep us safe online.

originalTitle: 'Superhuman' managers shouldn't have to ask for help - Beard's family on loss
title: When Grown-Ups Need a Helping Hand: A Lesson from Football!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, ready to share an important story with you today. It's all about football, big feelings, and why it's super important to be kind and help each other, especially the grown-ups who work so hard!

What Does a Football Coach Do?

Do you love watching football? Many of you do, I know! When you see a football team play, you often see a person on the side shouting instructions, clapping, and cheering. This person is called a coach, or sometimes a manager. Their job is super important! They teach the players how to kick the ball, how to work together, and how to be a strong team. Imagine you are building a big tower with blocks. Your coach helps you decide which blocks to use and how to stack them so your tower doesn't fall. That's a bit like what a football coach does for a team! They plan games, help players get better, and try to make sure everyone is happy and ready to play. Did you know? In Africa, football is one of the most loved sports! Many children dream of becoming a famous footballer or even a coach one day!

A Story About a Coach Named Matt

There was a kind man named Matt Beard. He was a very good football coach for many teams, helping players learn and grow. Matt worked very, very hard, and he loved football a lot. But sometimes, when grown-ups work super hard, they can feel a lot of heavy feelings inside. These feelings can be like carrying a very big backpack that gets heavier and heavier each day. It can make them feel tired, worried, or even sad. Matt's family, his wife and children, loved him very much. They saw how much effort he put into his job. They also saw that sometimes, even though he looked strong on the outside, he was carrying a lot of those heavy feelings inside. Now, Matt is not with his family anymore, and they miss him terribly. They want to share an important message with everyone.

Big Feelings Are For Everyone

Sometimes, we think grown-ups are like superheroes, always strong and always happy. But guess what? Grown-ups have feelings too! Just like you might feel sad if your toy breaks, or happy when you get a treat, grown-ups feel many different things. Coaches, like Matt, have to make many big decisions. They worry about their team, they worry about winning or losing, and they worry about all the players. This can make their inside feelings very heavy. It's like having many, many puzzles to solve all at once! Even if they smile on the outside, they might be feeling a bit overwhelmed inside. Did you know? Even the strongest person you know, like a parent or a teacher, feels worried or sad sometimes. It's a normal part of being a person!

Why Asking for Help is Super Power!

Matt's family said that sometimes, grown-ups think they have to be 'superhuman'. This means they think they should be strong all the time and never, ever ask for help. But the family wants everyone to know that this is not true! Asking for help is not a sign of weakness; it's a sign of bravery and strength! Imagine you are building that block tower, and it's getting too tall and wobbly. If you ask a friend to hold one side, or ask a grown-up for a different block, that's being smart, not weak! It helps your tower stay strong. It's the same with our feelings. If we feel those heavy feelings, talking to someone we trust – like a parent, a teacher, or a friend – can make that heavy backpack feel much lighter. Did you know? Sharing your feelings with someone you trust can actually make you feel better! It's like letting out a big sigh.

What Matt's Family Wants Us to Do

Matt's family wants people to understand that it's okay for coaches, and for everyone, to say, "I need a little help today." They want workplaces to be places where people feel safe to talk about their feelings and where everyone is kind to each other. They want to make sure that no other family has to go through the sadness they feel. They hope that by sharing Matt's story, more people will learn to look out for each other and offer a helping hand or a listening ear.

What Can We Do?

Even though you are young, you can help too! How? 1. **Be kind:** Always be kind to your friends, your family, and your teachers. A kind word or a smile can make a big difference. 2. **Talk about your feelings:** If you feel sad, worried, or even super happy, share it with a grown-up you trust. They want to hear from you! 3. **Listen to others:** If a friend looks sad, ask them if they are okay. Sometimes, just listening helps a lot. 4. **Remember it's okay to ask for help:** If your homework is too hard, or you can't reach something, ask for help! Grown-ups are there to help you. Let's all remember Matt's story and try to be kind, talk about our feelings, and help each other. Because being truly strong means knowing when to ask for a helping hand. And that's a goal worth scoring! Did you know? A simple "Are you okay?" can sometimes brighten someone's whole day!
summary: This story teaches us that even grown-up football coaches can have heavy feelings and it's brave and strong to ask for help, just like Matt Beard's family wants everyone to remember.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us that everyone, even strong grown-ups like football coaches, can feel sad or worried, and it's okay and good to ask for help and be kind to each other.

originalTitle: Spurs drag out safety battle to keep nerves jangling
title: Spurs drag out safety battle to keep nerves jangling
content: Tottenham hit the self-destruct button once more to leave Roberto de Zerbi frustrated as fight to avoid relegation looks set to go to the wire, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: Southampton ask for more time over spying claims
title: Southampton ask for more time over spying claims
content: Southampton request more time to conduct an internal review after being charged with spying on Championship play-off rivals Middlesbrough.
